title: quercus robur subsp. longipes
intro: Quercus robur subsp. longipes, also known as English oak, is a subspecies of the pedunculate oak tree native to Europe. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in reducing inflammation and acting as an astringent. The health benefits of Quercus robur subsp. longipes include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus robur subsp. longipes include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
